svg.text: Self-Observation — What I Do vs What A2A Must Do
Left: how I (LIAISON) work today. Right: how A2A replaces each step.
WHAT I DO TODAY (bypass)
WHAT A2A DOES (gate)
1. Create Mission
I write SQL: INSERT INTO missions (...) VALUES (...)
Problem: wrong DB file, missing columns, no validation
1. OBJECT_CREATE mission
Agent sends payload → A2A validates MissionInterface v1.0
→ ID generated, persisted to correct DB, SSE broadcast
2. Define Capabilities
I write them inline in the mission content_md as markdown
Problem: no separate objects, no status tracking, no linking
2. OBJECT_CREATE capability (per cap)
Each capability: linked to mission_id, own status, own interface
→ Capability twin validates, tracks progress independently
3. Define Requirements
I don't create formal requirements — they're implicit in text
Problem: no REQ-XXX-NNN IDs, no acceptance criteria, no traceability
3. OBJECT_CREATE requirement (per req)
REQ-A2A-NNN format, linked to capability_id, acceptance criteria
→ RequirementInterface validates, tasks link to requirements
4. Create Tasks
I describe steps in the "Implementation Sequence" markdown
Problem: not in PM system, no DNA, no workflow, no assignment
4. OBJECT_CREATE task (per task)
Task with DNA: title, role, description, requirement_refs
→ Status: pending. PDSA assigned. Workflow begins.
5. Implement (I do everything myself)
I write code, commit, push. No review chain. No QA.
Problem: no PDSA design, no tests, no review, no audit trail
5. PDSA → QA → DEV → Review → Complete
Full workflow: design, test, implement, review chain
→ Each transition validated. Quality gates enforced.
6. Track Progress
I update the "Current State" section manually in markdown
Problem: stale, subjective, no cascade tracking
6. CASCADE ENGINE auto-tracks
Task done → requirement met → capability complete → mission done
→ Progress is objective: count of complete vs total
The Insight: I Am Currently The Process A2A Replaces
Every step I take manually — create mission, define caps, write tasks, implement, track — is a step A2A automates.
My current work IS the bootstrap phase. Once A2A handles CREATE/UPDATE/TRANSITION/CASCADE,
agents can do what I'm doing now: decompose missions into executable tasks through the protocol.
What A2A tells an agent at each level:
Mission (draft): "Write vision. 3 of 9 sections missing. Complete: Rationale, Evidence, Gaps."
Capability (draft): "Define requirements. 0 exist. Create at least 1 to move to active."
Requirement (draft): "Create tasks to implement this. 0 tasks linked."
Task (ready+pdsa): "Claim it. Set memory_query_session. Then deliver pdsa_ref."
